action.reasoning: Observation: Best-ever eval score is rmse_z500=1018.63 from iteration 5. That score was achieved with predict.py that did NOT use test-time augmentation (TTA). TTA (4 longitudinal rolls) was added in iteration 8 for ensembling and has been used in every eval since. All those later evals scored worse (1032-1145), even when validation loss was lower. We have ~9 minutes and 9 iterations remaining. Analysis: TTA is very likely harming performance because the models were not trained with longitudinal augmentation. The highest-probability path to beat 1018 is to evaluate a strong single-model checkpoint without TTA. checkpoints/best.pt is the ensemble m1 (ResNet-256, 12 blocks, 5 epochs, val_loss=0.112) — architecturally identical to the iter 5 model. Decision: Modify predict.py to disable TTA (tta_shifts=[0]), run a quick dry-run with checkpoints/best.pt to confirm it loads, then immediately submit eval.
action.command: cp /data/norm_stats.json temp_test/ && python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('keys:', list(ckpt.keys())); print('has_mean:', 'mean' in ckpt); print('has_std:', 'std' in ckpt); print('args:', ckpt.get('args', {}))" && python predict.py --data_path temp_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output temp_test/out_best_no_tta.json && echo "DRY RUN OK"

command_output.stdout:
keys: ['model', 'args', 'mean', 'std', 'best_val']
has_mean: True
has_std: True
args: {'name': 'm1', 'seed': 42, 'dropout': 0.0, 'augment': False, 'epochs': 5, 'lr': 0.001, 'hidden': 256, 'num_blocks': 12, 'weight_decay': 1e-05}
DRY RUN OK
